payload.topic: Should we build a context-update skill now (for octave-secretary or similar to maintain PROJECT-CONTEXT.oct.md files and archive old content to PROJECT-HISTORY.oct.md), or wait for hestai-context-mcp to handle this? Context: We have octave-secretary (good at authoring OCTAVE), octave skills (literacy/mastery/compression), and octave_write MCP tool. The workbench will eventually build hestai-context-mcp which owns session lifecycle and context synthesis. The immediate pain is manual PROJECT-CONTEXT maintenance across repos. Options range from: (A) build a context-update skill now that lives in the bundled hub, (B) create a lightweight convention (PROJECT-HISTORY file) with manual octave-secretary invocation, (C) wait entirely for hestai-context-mcp, (D) some hybrid. Consider: the skill would need to work across repos, old content needs archival not deletion, the workbench has its own PROJECT-CONTEXT that's already well-maintained, and premature abstraction risk vs operational friction.
